heading: Unauthorized recording of Motion pictures in a Motion picture exhibition facility
label: 2319B Unauthorized recording of Motion pictures in a Motion picture exhibition facility
notes: References in TextThe Federal Rules of Criminal Procedure, referred to in subsec. (e)(1), are set out in the Appendix to this title.

Amendments2008—Subsec. (b). amended subsec. (b) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “When a person is convicted of a violation of subsection (a), the court in its judgment of conviction shall, in addition to any penalty provided, order the forfeiture and destruction or other disposition of all unauthorized copies of motion pictures or other audiovisual works protected under title 17, or parts thereof, and any audiovisual recording devices or other equipment used in connection with the offense.”Pub. L. 110–403
amendments: Amendments2008—Subsec. (b). amended subsec. (b) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “When a person is convicted of a violation of subsection (a), the court in its judgment of conviction shall, in addition to any penalty provided, order the forfeiture and destruction or other disposition of all unauthorized copies of motion pictures or other audiovisual works protected under title 17, or parts thereof, and any audiovisual recording devices or other equipment used in connection with the offense.”Pub. L. 110–403

children.text: be imprisoned for not more than 3 years, fined under this title, or both; or
if the offense is a second or subsequent offense, be imprisoned for no more than 6 years, fined under this title, or both.

children.heading: .—Forfeiture and Destruction of Property; Restitution
children.label: b .—Forfeiture and Destruction of Property; Restitution
children.text: Forfeiture, destruction, and restitution relating to this section shall be subject to section 2323, to the extent provided in that section, in addition to any other similar remedies provided by law.

children.heading: .—Authorized Activities
children.label: c .—Authorized Activities
children.text: This section does not prevent any lawfully authorized investigative, protective, or intelligence activity by an officer, agent, or employee of the United States, a State, or a political subdivision of a State, or by a person acting under a contract with the United States, a State, or a political subdivision of a State.

may detain, in a reasonable manner and for a reasonable time, any person suspected of a violation of this section with respect to that motion picture or audiovisual work for the purpose of questioning or summoning a law enforcement officer; and
shall not be held liable in any civil or criminal action arising out of a detention under paragraph (1).

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: During the preparation of the presentence report under rule 32(c) of the Federal Rules of Criminal Procedure, victims of an offense under this section shall be permitted to submit to the probation officer a victim impact statement that identifies the victim of the offense and the extent and scope of the injury and loss suffered by the victim, including the estimated economic impact of the offense on that victim.

children.label: A
children.text: producers and sellers of legitimate works affected by conduct involved in the offense;

children.label: B
children.text: holders of intellectual property rights in the works described in subparagraph (A); and

children.label: C
children.text: the legal representatives of such producers, sellers, and holders.

children.heading: .—State Law Not Preempted
children.label: f .—State Law Not Preempted
children.text: Nothing in this section may be construed to annul or limit any rights or remedies under the laws of any State.

children.heading: .—Title 17 definitions
children.label: 1 .—Title 17 definitions
children.text: The terms “audiovisual work”, “copy”, “copyright owner”, “motion picture”, “motion picture exhibition facility”, and “transmit” have, respectively, the meanings given those terms in .section 101 of title 17

children.heading: .—Audiovisual recording device
children.label: 2 .—Audiovisual recording device
children.text: The term “audiovisual recording device” means a digital or analog photographic or video camera, or any other technology or device capable of enabling the recording or transmission of a copyrighted motion picture or other audiovisual work, or any part thereof, regardless of whether audiovisual recording is the sole or primary purpose of the device.
